channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Local Multimodal LLM on iOS with `llama.cpp` (Swift + ObjC++)
item.description: I want a real local pipeline: image in, structured JSON out, no cloud dependency. Optimized to run Metal / ANE or whatever apple exposes ? My goal is to infer a json-struct of variables from image...

item.title: Source Score: Using AI to automate addition of new sources
item.description: This post is a continuation of a microservice I've been building. You can check out my last post in the series here. Automate News Source Ingestion with Firecrawl, OpenRouter, and GitHub Actions...

item.title: Windows 11 is getting a macOS-like speed boost
item.description: Microsoft is currently testing a new speed boost feature in Windows 11 that is designed to improve app launch times and make things like the Start menu feel more responsive. The feature, which is...

item.title: How I Reduced My Cloud Bill by 60% Without Touching My Code
item.description: Last year I was paying $340/month for infrastructure that served ~50k monthly active users. Today I pay $136/month for the same load. Here's exactly what I changed — zero code rewrites required....

item.title: 10 AI Tools That Actually Save Developers Time in 2026
item.description: AI tools are everywhere — but most of them are hype. After testing dozens of tools in real projects, here are the 10 that actually save me time every single day as a developer. 1. 🤖 Cursor IDE...

item.title: How Claude Code Improved My LinkedIn Profile Visibility !!
item.description: A practical 4-minute case study on using Claude Code to review, restructure, and optimise a LinkedIn profile for better visibility, recruiter discovery, and professional positioning. LinkedIn is...

item.title: Organized Dogmatism Controls the Message about Gender Bias in the Academy
item.description: Article URL: https://journalofcontroversialideas.org/article/6/1/313 Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48093468 Points: 10 # Comments: 1

item.title: 6 years building Planning Poker Online: from a weekend project to 147K users (and 3 mistakes I had to fix)
item.description: In early 2020 I built a real-time planning poker app over a weekend, almost by accident. Six years later, 147,000+ people use it every month, including teams at Google. This is a story about...

item.title: I Work in Hollywood. Everyone Who Used to Make TV Is Now Training AI

item.title: LLMs and Text-in-Text Steganography
item.description: Turns out that LLMs are really good at hiding text messages in other text messages.

item.title: Improving First Byte and Contentful Paint on a Django Website
item.description: Recently I have been experimenting with http streaming and realized how it can improve page performance. If you come from the PHP world, you might know the command flush(). It immediately sends to...

item.title: WEEK 3 #100DAYSOFSOLANA: SHARING MY TOOL AND OUTPUT
item.description: When I first started learning Solana, transactions felt like magic. You click "Send", wait a few seconds, and somehow value moves across a decentralised network. But after spending time building...

item.title: Forza Horizon 6 has been leaked and cracked a week before its release
item.description: The full version of Playground Games' upcoming Forza Horizon 6 game has leaked online over the weekend and has already been cracked by pirates. The download appeared on file sharing sites after...

item.title: CUDA Proves Nvidia Is a Software Company
item.description: There’s a deep, forbidding moat that surrounds Nvidia—and it has nothing to do with hardware.

item.title: I Work in Hollywood. Everyone Who Used to Make TV Is Now Secretly Training AI
item.description: For screenwriters like me—and job seekers all over—AI gig work is the new waiting tables. In eight months, I’ve done 20 of these soul-crushing contracts for five different platforms. It’s bad.

item.title: OpenAI Campus Network: Student club interest form
item.description: Join the OpenAI Campus Network—connect student clubs worldwide, access AI tools, host events, and build an AI-powered campus community.

item.title: How enterprises are scaling AI
item.description: How enterprises scale AI: from early experiments to compounding impact through trust, governance, workflow design, and quality at scale.

item.title: Logitech’s tiny folding mouse improves upon the laptop trackpad
item.description: Logitech is reportedly developing a new wireless mouse that folds in half to make it easier to carry around in a bag or pocket. According to leaked marketing images shared by WinFuture, Logitech's...

item.title: Fake OpenAI Privacy Filter Repo Hits #1 on Hugging Face, Draws 244K Downloads
item.description: A malicious Hugging Face repository managed to take a spot in the platform's trending list by impersonating OpenAI's Privacy Filter open-weight model to deliver a Rust-based information stealer to...

item.title: The new AI-powered Google Finance is expanding to Europe.
item.description: A screenshot of the AI-powered experience on Google Finance.

item.title: Get ready for the whisper-filled office of the future
item.description: How will work setups change if we spend more and more time talking to our computers?

item.title: The Bastl Kalimba is a wild synth that thinks it’s a thumb piano
item.description: Make no mistake, the Bastl Kalimba is a synthesizer, you just play it like a kalimba. Its tines don't really make much sound. There is an internal mic that you can blend in for a little acoustic...

item.title: Anthropic says ‘evil’ portrayals of AI were responsible for Claude’s blackmail attempts
item.description: Fictional portrayals of artificial intelligence can have a real effect on AI models, according to Anthropic.

item.title: MachinaCheck: Building a Multi-Agent CNC Manufacturability System on AMD MI300X
Ashnymph’s Childhood EP is exhilarating dance goth rock
item.description: I've got to thank my oldest friend and concert buddy, Tim, for turning me on to this one. Ashnymph is a London band that blends post-punk melodies with Krautrock rhythms and industrial grime....

item.title: We’re feeling cynical about xAI’s big deal with Anthropic
item.description: On the latest episode of the Equity podcast, we discussed what xAI's deal with Anthropic might mean for parent company SpaceX.

item.title: Cricut’s $99 craft cutting machine helped me feel creative again
item.description: I've always been skeptical of products that claim to help you lead a more creative life. But one recently won me over. I've spent three weeks with the Cricut Joy 2, a smart cutting and drawing...

item.title: Writers are fleeing the Substack Tax
item.description: Substack, the once buzzy newsletter platform, is losing a new swath of writers to rival platforms most people haven't heard of. Just last month, The Ankler, one of Substack's most popular...

item.title: Ollama Out-of-Bounds Read Vulnerability Allows Remote Process Memory Leak
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a critical security vulnerability in Ollama that, if successfully exploited, could allow a remote, unauthenticated attacker to leak its entire process...

item.title: Netflix may have finally figured out games
item.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on Netflix, follow Andrew Webster. The Stepback arrives in our subscribers' inboxes at 8AM...
